content.problemStatement:
Sereja has an array **A** that contains **n** integers, namely **A1, A2, ..., An**. A single action consists of performing one of two following operations:


To swap two consecutive elements;
To make a random and uniform shuffle of all the elements of the array **A**.
Now Sereja's goal is to make the expected value of the function **f(A)** as minimal as possible, by doing no more than **k** actions.

The function **f(A)** is the minimal number of consecutive elements' swaps you need in order to sort the array **A** in the increasing order.

What is the expected value of **f(A)** in case Sereja uses no more than **k** actions in the optimal way?
constraints: All values of Ai will form a permutation of the first N natural numbers
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two integers N and K on the first line, followed by N integers on the second line.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimal possible expected value of f(A) on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the array is already sorted, so the expected value is 0. For the second test case, the array is not sorted, and we need to perform one action to sort it. The expected value is the number of inversions divided by the length of the array.
The array is not sorted, and we need to perform two actions to sort it. The expected value is the number of inversions divided by the length of the array.
The array is not sorted, and we need to perform three actions to sort it. The expected value is the number of inversions divided by the length of the array.

solution.approach: The key insight is to calculate the expected value of the function f(A) by considering all possible permutations of the array A. We can use the concept of linearity of expectation to simplify the calculation. The algorithm used is to calculate the number of inversions in the array A and then use this value to calculate the expected value of f(A).
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^2) per test case, O(T * N^2) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the array A.
